## How to use a venv to avoid problems with python

### On Windows

`python -m venv venv`

`.\venv\Scripts\Activate.ps1`
  on PS
    if error
        - only this time: `Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope Process -ExecutionPolicy Bypass`
        - permanent: `Set-ExecutionPolicy -Scope CurrentUser -ExecutionPolicy RemoteSigned`
OR
- `.\venv\Scripts\activate.bat`
  on CMD, does not work on PS

then
`pip install pre-commit`
or better
`python -m pip install -r csProjectManager/requirements.txt`
or
`pip install -r csProjectManager/requirements.txt`


#### Avoid Python from Microsoft Store

1️⃣ Uninstall Python from Microsoft Store

Option A — Settings (recommended)

• Settings → Apps → Installed apps
• Search for **Python**
• Remove any entry that says **“Python 3.x (Microsoft Store)”**
• Restart PowerShell


2️⃣ Disable Python execution aliases (CRITICAL ⚠️)

Even after uninstalling, execution aliases may remain active.

• Settings → Apps → Advanced app settings
• App execution aliases

Turn **OFF**:
- python.exe
- python3.exe
- pip.exe
- pip3.exe

➡️ This is the **most important step**


3️⃣ Verify Store Python is gone

Open a new PowerShell and run:

where python

✅ Good result:
C:\Users\...\Programs\Python\Python314\python.exe

❌ Bad result (Store stub):
...\Microsoft\WindowsApps\python.exe

Also check:

python --version


4️⃣ Ensure real Python is first in PATH

Your PATH should contain entries like:

C:\Users\<you>\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python314\
C:\Users\<you>\AppData\Local\Programs\Python\Python314\Scripts\

And **must NOT** rely on WindowsApps aliases.

You can verify with:

Get-Command python


5️⃣ Recreate virtual environments (IMPORTANT 🧪)

Any virtual environment created while Store Python was active is **tainted**.

Delete and recreate:

rmdir /s /q venv
python -m venv venv

Then activate the venv and reinstall dependencies.


6️⃣ Download and install official Python (recommended 🐍)

• Go to https://www.python.org/downloads/windows/
• Download **Windows installer (64-bit)**

During installation:
- ✅ Check **“Add Python to PATH”**
- ✅ Choose **“Install for me only”**
- ⛔ Avoid Microsoft Store installer
- Optional: disable **“Install launcher for all users”**

After installation, open a new PowerShell and verify:

python --version
where python


7️⃣ Final sanity check ✅

where python
where pip
python -c "import sys; print(sys.executable)"

All outputs should point to:

...\Programs\Python\Python314\...1️⃣ Uninstall Python from Microsoft Store

where pip

All outputs should point to:

...\Programs\Python\Python314\...

### On Linux:

`python -m venv venv`

`source venv/bin/activate`

## How To Setup Secrects in Github to allow actions to run and local download of precompiled binaries

The metaproject uses a standard named `secret` to allow access to repository

In order to setup it, follow the next steps:

### Generate a new secret

- go to https://github.com/settings/tokens or navigate to it via `user icon (on top right)` -> `settings` -> `<> Developer Settings` -> `Personal Access Tokens` -> `Tokens (classic)`
- `Generate new token` -> `Generate new token (classic)`
- Add a `Note` (e.g. you meta project name)
- Select a Expiration
- Add `repo` checkbox
- Add `read:packages` too
- `Generate token` (page bottom)

Make sure to copy the token (usually it starts with `ghp_...`)

### Add the token to the meta-project

- Go to the repository of this meta-project
- `Settings` -> `Secrects and variables` -> `Actions`
- In `Repository secrets` create a `New Repository Settings`
- Name it `ACTIONS_ORG_ACCESS` and paste the token created at the step above

Manually re-run the workflows or wait for a auto triggered build

### Add the token to your local config to allow download of precompiled binaries

`pip install keyring`

`python3 -m keyring set github csCosineGithubToken`
--> enter the token
